paper: Corporate and Other Laws  |  Master Test 01

steps.for: scheme 2 - subsidiary of same holding company is related party
scheme 1 - s.16 name resembling TM proprietor complaint
scheme 2 - 3 years window correct, 3 months resolution wrong
scheme f - cannot be withdrawn after declaration
scheme 1 - bank loan not a deposit
scheme 3 - applied to Sohan and Subarna, partial

crosses.for: his wrong 25% limit for bonus shares
his wrong statement that s.67 applies to both

remarks.title: Part I
lines: 13 of 15. Q5 wrong, all other seven correct. On Q5 you marked (D) but the correct
answer is (C) - a Government company is not eligible to accept/renew deposits beyond
35% of paid-up share capital, free reserves, and securities premium.

remarks.title: Related party / wholly owned subsidiaries
lines: Earned: 1 mark for stating that subsidiaries of the same holding company are related
parties (clause B).
Not earned: You missed mentioning that clause (viii) does not apply to s.188 for a
private company per Notification G.S.R. 464(E). Therefore, your conclusion that they
are related parties for the purpose of s.188 was incorrect.

remarks.title: Company name / Trade Marks Act
lines: Earned: s.16 provision described generally, 3-year window for TM proprietor complaint,
name is identical to the trademark, and that the complaint is within time.
Not earned: 0.5 marks because you wrote name must be changed within 30 days of
direction (instead of 3 months) and missed that it requires an ordinary resolution.
Warning: Section 16 is not explicitly named. ROC is mentioned as directing the change
in the provision, though CG/RD is the correct authority (which you correctly named
in the conclusion).

remarks.title: Expert liability for misstatement in prospectus
lines: Earned: Stating the general civil liability under s.35(1) (0.5 marks), expert not liable
if consent withdrawn before issue (0.5 marks), and prospectus issued without knowledge
(0.5 marks).
Not earned: No application or conclusion written for Mr. Alok. You also missed the
requirements of correct and fair representation, expert competence, and consent not
withdrawn for the third exemption.
Warning: You cite Section 34 (criminal liability) and Section 477 (which is fraud,
though the correct section is 447). Civil liability is governed by Section 35.

remarks.title: Bonus shares
lines: Earned: Pre-requisites of Articles authorization (1 mark), General Meeting approval
(1 mark), and that declared bonus shares cannot be withdrawn (1 mark).
Not earned: You did not mention other pre-requisites like no default in debt
interest/principal, no default in employee dues, and making partly paid shares fully paid.
Warning: You cite Section 62 (which is for further issue of capital) instead of Section 63.
You also wrote incorrect percentage limits (15%, 25%) that do not apply to bonus shares.
Special resolution is cited instead of ordinary resolution.

remarks.title: Deposits
lines: Earned: Part (a) in full - bank loan is not a deposit and Mr. Taar's contention is
incorrect. Part (b) in full - Government company deposit limit is 35% and the 25%
limit is incorrect.
Warning: You cite Section 2(1)(c) instead of Rule 2(1)(c) for deposit exclusions.

remarks.title: Financial assistance to buy own shares
lines: Earned: s.67(2) financial assistance prohibition (1 mark), s.67(3) employee exception
(1 mark), correct application to Sohan and Subarna (0.5 marks), and correct conclusion
that the answer remains the same in part (ii) (1 mark).
Not earned: Under part (ii), your reasoning is incorrect (stating that s.67 applies to
private companies generally).
Warning: Section 67 is not named. You wrote 'financial instance' instead of 'financial
assistance'. You missed explaining that SSPL is a deemed public company u/s 2(71)
in part (i).

remarks.title: Employee security deposit
lines: Earned: Citing Rule 2(1)(c) (1 mark), stating the employee security deposit exemption
conditions (2 marks), and applying to Mr. Romit (2 marks) for a perfect score.

rating.name: Introduction Competence
rating.note: You open with the provisions but frequently omit the exact section numbers (e.g. s.16, s.63, s.67 are missing, and wrong ones like s.34, s.477, s.62 are cited).

rating.name: Content Coverage
rating.note: Good coverage on the attempted chapters. Missed several step marks in Q1(a) and Q1(c) (no application for Mr. Alok was written).

rating.name: Conclusion Competence
rating.note: Most conclusions are correct, though Q1(a) has a wrong conclusion due to missing private company exemption, and Q4(a)(ii) has wrong reasoning.

rating.name: Presentation & Structure
rating.note: Outstanding presentation. Points are clean, handwriting is neat, well-spaced, with clear Separation of Provision and Conclusion.

rating.name: Language Proficiency
rating.note: Clear, correct legal terminology. Watch spelling of words like 'financial instance' (assistance), 'relivent' (relevant), and 'reciept' (receipt).

feedback.head: WHAT YOU DID WELL
lines: Your presentation and handwriting are outstanding. The pointwise structure, clear headings,
and neat margins make your paper extremely easy to read and evaluate.
Excellent score in deposits (Q2(b) and Q4(b) both 5/5) showing thorough conceptual clarity.
Good application of s.67(3) employee exceptions in Q4(a) for Mr. Sohan and Mrs. Subarna.

feedback.head: HABITS TO FIX
lines: *1. Quote correct section numbers.
You missed quoting Section 16 (Q1(b)), Section 63 (Q2(a), cited 62 instead), and Section 67
(Q4(a)). You also cited Section 34 and 477 instead of Section 35 and 447 (Q1(c)). Memorize the
exact section numbers as they are critical.
*2. Do not leave descriptive questions incomplete.
In Q1(c), you wrote the exemptions for expert liability but completely forgot to apply them to
Alok or write a conclusion. Always ensure you answer the specific scenario asked in the question.
*3. Double check reasoning.
In Q4(a)(ii), Section 67 doesn't apply to private companies generally (they are exempt unless
they violate the exemption conditions, which SSPL did).
*4. Start every new question from a new page.
Always start Question 2, Question 3, etc. on a fresh page rather than continuing on the same page.
*5. Write all sub-parts of a question together.
Group all sub-parts (e.g., 1(a), 1(b), 1(c)) together. Do not mix them with other questions. If in
doubt, leave enough space to write the answer later to maintain visibility.

feedback.head: STRATEGY FOR NEXT EXAM
lines: You attempted 35 marks of descriptive questions and scored 23.5 (67%).
With your strong presentation and good command of provisions, focusing on memorizing
section numbers and completing the application parts of questions will easily push you
past 40+ marks.
Excellent effort, keep it up!

footer: If you want to go deeper on any of this, you can connect with an SME.
